title: Ryūtōsan Shrine
summary: Ryūtōsan Shrine (龍頭山神社) was a Shinto shrine in Korea. It was the earliest shinto Shinto shrine built in Korea.
text:
Ryūtōsan Shrine (龍頭山神社) was a Shinto shrine in Korea. It was the earliest shinto Shinto shrine built in Korea.

History
Originally named Kotohira shrine (金刀比羅神社), the shrine was built by workers of the local Japan House trade office in 1678. Shinto shrines of this kind may have served the purpose of expressing and maintaining a Japanese identity outside of Japan, while the Japanese and Korean people lived relatively closely during this period. it was later renamed Ryūtōsan Shrine (龍頭山神社, Ryūtōsan Jinja).
The shrine was destroyed after the liberation of Korea. Its former site is now part of Yongdusan Park.

Enshrined deities
The shrine was a Kotohira shrine, dedicated to Ōmononushi and the protection of sailors. In addition it, alongside all other nationally ranked shinto shrines in Korea, enshrined Amaterasu and Kunitama as a pair.
